content:
Edgewonk Setup Guide: Complete Tutorial for Traders

What is Edgewonk?

Edgewonk is a data-driven trading journal designed specifically for traders who want to identify and refine their trading edge through detailed analytics and psychology tracking. Founded in 2014 and based in Vienna, Austria, Edgewonk stands out with its single-tier pricing model ($197/year for 12-month billing or $297 for 24-month billing) and a focused feature set built around statistical edge discovery. With a 4.4/5 rating, the platform appeals most to analytical traders, forex specialists, and anyone serious about understanding the psychological patterns that impact their trading performance. Unlike Tradervue and Myfxbook, Edgewonk's unique Tiltmeter feature detects emotional trading patterns in real-time, while its trade simulator lets you test "what-if" scenarios without risking capital.

How to Create Your Edgewonk Account

Getting started with Edgewonk requires just a few minutes and minimal information. Here's the step-by-step process:

  • Visit the Edgewonk website and locate the sign-up button. You'll be prompted to enter your email address and create a password. Keep your password secure—this account will contain all your trading data.
  • Verify your email address by clicking the confirmation link sent to your inbox. Check your spam folder if you don't see it within a few minutes.
  • Choose your billing plan during account creation. Edgewonk offers only annual billing: either 12 months at $16.42/month ($197 total) or 24 months at $12.38/month ($297 total). The 24-month plan provides the best value at nearly 27% savings per year.
  • Enter payment information securely. Edgewonk accepts major credit cards and processes through standard payment gateways.
  • Complete your profile setup by selecting your primary trading markets (forex, commodities, indices, options, stocks, crypto, or futures). You can modify this later.
  • Estimated setup time: 5-10 minutes. Unlike platforms offering free trials, Edgewonk requires immediate commitment, so ensure this aligns with your needs before signing up.

Note: Edgewonk's lack of a free tier means you won't test the platform risk-free, which is a legitimate concern for new traders. Budget accordingly and consider whether the single-tier model justifies the upfront investment.

Setting Up Edgewonk for the First Time

Once you've created your account, the initial configuration is crucial to getting accurate data and meaningful analytics. Start by exploring the dashboard—Edgewonk displays your trade statistics, performance metrics, and psychological indicators in one central location, though users note the interface feels dated compared to newer platforms.

Connect your broker or data feed: Edgewonk integrates with most major brokers. In the settings menu, look for "Broker Integration" and select your broker from the available list. You'll typically need to provide API credentials or authorize a connection. This step is essential for automatic trade logging and real-time data syncing.

Configure your account preferences: Set your account currency, trading hours, and timezone. These settings affect how Edgewonk calculates returns, risk metrics, and performance statistics. If you trade multiple sessions across different markets (e.g., forex during London hours and stocks during New York hours), configure each timezone appropriately.

Set your risk parameters: Define your maximum acceptable daily loss, position sizing rules, and risk-per-trade targets. This information feeds into Edgewonk's risk management and performance analytics features, helping you identify when you're deviating from your planned risk rules.

Review the Tiltmeter settings: This unique feature monitors for emotional trading patterns. Configure what behaviors trigger a "tilt" alert—sudden position size increases, revenge trading, or consecutive losses often indicate emotional decision-making. The Tiltmeter acts as an early warning system for psychological breakdown.

Estimated setup time: 15-20 minutes. Having accurate initial configuration saves hours debugging data inconsistencies later.

Essential Features You Should Know

1. Trade Journaling with Performance Analytics

Edgewonk's core feature is its comprehensive trade journal. Every trade you log includes entry price, exit price, position size, market conditions, and your reasoning. The platform then calculates your win rate, average win/loss, profit factor, and Sharpe ratio automatically. Unlike generic journals, Edgewonk links these metrics directly to your decision-making patterns, showing you which strategies actually work versus which ones you're emotionally attached to.

2. Tiltmeter (Emotional Trading Detection)

The Tiltmeter tracks emotional deterioration during live trading. It flags unusual position sizes, rapid-fire trades, consecutive losses, and other stress-response patterns. This feature addresses a critical gap in other journals like Tradervue—most platforms track what happened, but Edgewonk predicts when your psychology is likely to fail. Review your Tiltmeter reports weekly to identify stress triggers and develop countermeasures.

3. Trade Simulator (What-If Analysis)

Use the simulator to test position size changes, different entry/exit rules, or strategy modifications against historical trades. Instead of risking real money on a hypothesis, run it against past market data first. This is particularly valuable for forex and futures traders testing edge refinements without live account risk.

4. Backtesting

Edgewonk's backtesting tool lets you run historical strategies across past market data. Input your entry and exit rules, position sizing, and risk parameters, then see how your approach would have performed. This bridges the gap between theoretical trading edge and actual market conditions. Combine backtesting results with simulator tests for confidence before implementing changes.

5. Broker Integration

Direct broker connections eliminate manual entry errors. Supported brokers automatically sync your trades into Edgewonk, updating performance metrics in real-time. If your broker isn't directly supported, you can manually log trades, though this reduces the time-saving advantage of the platform.

6. Risk Management Tools

Set daily loss limits, maximum position sizes, and risk-per-trade percentages. Edgewonk alerts you when you're approaching these limits and documents when you violate them, creating accountability. This is essential for preventing catastrophic losses during emotional trading periods flagged by the Tiltmeter.

7. Education and Psychological Insights

Beyond tracking, Edgewonk provides educational resources on trade psychology and edge discovery. The platform emphasizes that consistent profitability depends on understanding your own trading patterns, not just market patterns. Review the education section monthly to reinforce psychological discipline.

Edgewonk Pricing: Which Plan Should You Choose?

Edgewonk operates on a single-tier model—all features are available to all users, regardless of plan. You're only choosing your billing commitment:

12-Month Annual Plan: $197/year ($16.42/month)

This is the entry-level option for traders wanting to commit for one year. It provides full access to journaling, Tiltmeter, backtesting, simulator, and all analytics features. Best for: traders new to systematic journaling who want to test the platform's value before longer commitment.

24-Month Annual Plan: $297 total ($12.38/month or $148.50/year)

This plan saves approximately 27% per year compared to the 12-month option and locks in your rate for two years. Best for: serious traders confident in their commitment to systematic trading and psychological development. The long-term pricing reflects Edgewonk's target audience: traders viewing the journal as essential infrastructure, not optional software.

Important caveat: Edgewonk offers no monthly billing and no free trial. This is a significant barrier for traders wanting to test before committing. Unlike platforms such as Tradervue, which offer trial periods, you must pay upfront. For $197-$297 annually, the commitment is modest, but it eliminates risk-free evaluation.

Recommendation matrix:

  • Beginner traders: Consider the 12-month plan to test whether systematic journaling changes your results.
  • Intermediate traders: The 24-month plan makes sense if you're already journaling manually and see clear ROI potential.
  • Advanced/professional traders: The 24-month plan is a no-brainer given the platform's focus on edge discovery and psychology—both accelerators of performance improvement.

Pro Tips for Getting the Most Out of Edgewonk

1. Log every trade, not just winners. Edgewonk's edge-finding algorithm requires complete data. Skipping small losses or breakeven trades introduces survivorship bias into your analysis and makes your metrics meaningless. Set a calendar reminder to log daily, or automate this via broker integration if your broker is supported.

2. Use the Tiltmeter as a leading indicator, not confirmation. Review your Tiltmeter score before market open, not after a bad day. If it's already elevated from previous sessions, plan a reduced position size or skip trading entirely. Catching tilt before it worsens beats recovering from emotional damage afterward.

3. Run the simulator on every strategy change. Before modifying your entry rules, position sizing, or timeframe, run it against 50+ historical trades. If your backtest shows improvement but the simulator shows your past self would have failed to execute it consistently, the change isn't an upgrade—it's a trap.

4. Set hard limits in the Risk Management section and honor them.** Edgewonk tracks violations; the data is irrelevant if you override limits. Your daily loss limit should be non-negotiable. This discipline separates traders who use Edgewonk for accountability from those using it as retrospective journaling theater.

5. Extract your monthly psychological profile.** Edgewonk generates reports on decision quality, consistency, and tilt patterns. Review these monthly to identify repeating weaknesses. If you always tilt on Mondays or after consecutive losses, design pre-planned responses now, not reactively during the event.

6. Compare your backtest to actual results.** If your simulator shows 55% win rate but actual trading produces 48%, something is degrading between plan and execution—usually psychology or slippage. Use this gap to calibrate the Tiltmeter's sensitivity and adjust risk parameters downward until simulated and actual results converge.

7. Revisit your broker integration monthly.** API connections occasionally break or drift. Verify that recent trades in Edgewonk match your broker statement exactly. A 1% data discrepancy snowballs into unreliable performance metrics across months.

Common Edgewonk Issues and How to Fix Them

Issue 1: "My broker isn't listed in the integration menu."

Solution: Edgewonk supports major brokers but not every small regional platform. Check the integration documentation on Edgewonk's website for the current list. If unsure, contact support with your broker's name. In the meantime, log trades manually—it's slower but preserves your data integrity. Some traders use Google Sheets to batch-export trades into Edgewonk weekly, splitting the difference between manual and automated logging.

Issue 2: "The Tiltmeter triggers on normal trades; I'm getting false alerts."

Solution: The Tiltmeter's sensitivity is configurable. If you're consistently getting alerts that don't match your actual psychological state, adjust the threshold in settings. However, resist the temptation to disable alerts entirely—that defeats the purpose. Instead, calibrate by comparing alerts to journal entries. If the Tiltmeter flagged a trade as emotional but your notes show sound reasoning, increase the sensitivity. But if it's often right when you're wrong, keep it as is.

Issue 3: "Data import shows missing or duplicate trades."

Solution: Broker integrations occasionally sync incomplete data, especially if you trade during market open or have overnight positions. Manually review your trade list against your broker statement monthly. Edgewonk allows bulk editing to correct discrepancies. If duplicates appear frequently, disconnect and reconnect your broker integration, which usually forces a clean re-sync.

Issue 4: "The interface feels clunky compared to newer platforms."

Solution: Edgewonk prioritizes functionality over aesthetic polish. The interface is dated, and that's a real usability drawback, but it doesn't affect the quality of your analytics. Learn the keyboard shortcuts to navigate faster, and batch your journal reviews weekly rather than checking daily—this reduces friction from the dated UI. If the interface becomes genuinely untenable, consider competitors like Myfxbook, though you'll sacrifice Edgewonk's depth in psychology tracking.

Is Edgewonk Worth It? Our Verdict

Edgewonk delivers genuine value if you're serious about systematic trading improvement and willing to commit to rigorous journaling. The Tiltmeter, trade simulator, and edge-discovery focus differentiate it meaningfully from generic journals; most traders treating trading as a business (not a hobby) see measurable ROI within 3-6 months of consistent use. At $197-$297 annually, the cost is negligible compared to potential profit improvement from better psychology management and edge refinement. However, the lack of a free trial, dated interface, and annual-only billing are legitimate drawbacks—if you're a casual trader or want to test drive before committing, start elsewhere. For analytical traders, forex specialists, and anyone serious about finding their statistical edge, Edgewonk is absolutely worth the investment. Start with the 12-month plan; upgrade to 24-month billing once you've confirmed the platform fits your workflow. For more detailed comparisons, see our full Edgewonk review.
author: TradingToolsHub Editorial
